And then what's the number one thing you see missing on people's websites nowadays that would help them get a position to sell? They don't evangelize the market leader content, right? I mean, you gotta think about this. You have nanoseconds. People are finding you all the time through your website. If you're not clearly communicating what we've covered in this one-page attack and conquer plan, you're not positioning market leader content. You're turning off prospects. And more importantly, if you're not scaling, you're turning off potential buyers. Listen, in the investment banking space, where people are out looking to find companies to buy and sell, they have analysts that spend hours a day searching companies. Where do they get their information from? The website. Okay, and if it's not fucking rock solid, guess who doesn't get a call?
So, like, if you take, you know, you should be taking a multitude of things away from this workshop. Go fix your house. And depending on how you optimize that or not will dictate your future. Go ahead. Last question. Are there any color do's and don'ts or like font do's and don'ts? Like, just kind of obvious things that you know people should not be doing? Yeah, I'm sure. Like, I mean, I would find, you know, it needs to load fast, needs to be responsive. I would do kind of website fonts. Right. I would stay away from a lot of.
Loading images, I would test. You know, there's you know, Jeremy, you could probably drop some resources in here on on colors and buttons and things. I mean, you know, we would always be optimized, we would let the data inform the outcome. Right. So we would test a red button versus a green button versus an orange button. I mean, we would try to stay on color of brand, but whatever the consumer resonated more with is the direction we went. And I think that's something important. Listen to guys. Remember the minute you think you're in the creative business, that's a shitty fucking business. It's subjective, right? But you'll get fired overnight. You think you have a great idea and you get home and the guy's wife, or, you know, the husband looks at it and goes, 'Oh, that looks like shit.' and now you're done, right?
Be in the business of generating revenue. Be in the business of using data to inform all the creative decisions you do, and you'll have more longevity in this business. All right, last question. So once again, about the perspective of people buying you out, there's all kinds of new tools and, like, funnel builders and shit like that right now that's available. Does that actually matter if your site is built on WordPress? Or can I be built on ClickFunnels or Instapage or whatever other tools? Yeah, I think technology is a commodity. I think what's more valuable is the content, the credibility, assets, or whatever. If you're selling some piece of tech, your site should be on than it should be. But a buyer is not going to care if it's built on ClickFunnels, WordPress, if it's open source, if it's .
NET. They just want to look at it. Is it a viable marketing asset that's generating qualified leads and helping assist in sales? All right, guys, thank you so much, man. I hope that was beneficial.
